title: Re: how do u guys beat bosses?
description:
You've only played this game then.

Dark Souls 1-3 and Elden Ring all can literally 1-shot all the bosses, have large numbers of skills that uber nuke and stunlock bosses, has far more accessible absurd EHP (effective health points, aka tanking ability), and more cheeses then perhaps any other game. Don't even get me started on the basic mobs that can almost all universally be stunlocked even at level 1 with a dagger until they die.

I do like those games, hence my beating them all at level 1 +0 upgrades multiple NG+s, etc. but you're being ridiculous with your claim. The only souls-like games that are in the same difficulty category are going to be Sekiro, and of course Ys at higher difficulties smashes them both.


You're stuck on the bald monk? Seriously?

I jest, it's a rite of passage. He's actually one of the harder bosses in the game, because his damage is very high for how early you encounter him compared to most bosses, you aren't experienced with the game yet, and your lack of abilities/fight options yet.

He can be beaten that early, definitely. Took me 3 tries as one of the bosses I actually died against, but took some people literal hours (somehow?) and then they found many others much easier after.

A tip if you didn't already find it, but there is a fire transformation from a nearby boss from the shrine facing towards the bald boss west of it. That form has its own separate full health bar and you can spam the dash move for avoiding dmg and higher dps than the basic attacks.

You can tagger the boss with either 2 focus attacks or the varied combos heavy attacks.

Alternatively, honestly if you just are annoyed with that boss go past it and come back later when you are a lot stronger. All you have to do is before you ring the 3rd bell unlocking (warping you) the secret area is to beat this boss. If not mistaken, you might be able to do it at any point of the game, but not 100% so I recommend just doing it before chapter 1's final boss the giant flaming bear > beat bald monk > ring 3rd bell and go to hidden temple and beat gold bald monk > then beat flaming bear.

The bald monk, Wandering Wright, is the strongest spirit in the game btw, but not early on. Early it's just okay. It's real strength comes as you get Cloud Step upgraded to give the huge crit chance boost (up to +50% on next single hit attack) and when also used with other buffs like Pigrim Set, etc. attack dmg boosts. It can nuke bosses and 1-shot them even. Early on while it is still not that strong it can be great at doing a nice boost of dmg and interrupting/knocking down bosses in the middle of their attacks. It can also break some cracked walls unlocking secrets...

The gold monk is much easier btw, almost impossible to lose to due to a special armor you get for that boss that lets you infinitely heal.
label: analytical

title: Re: how do u guys beat bosses?
description:
Uh, those runes are from the final boss.

It's how many he drops, because I died before them and I don't bother hanging onto misc runes I'm not using and own hundreds (mostly max amount) of all buyable items, own all limited quantity items, and have looted / beaten everything in the game. It's a 100% clear character, plus there is the farm in the underground blood area so I just basically don't care about souls I lose. So yeah, it's just the boss' reward souls which is exactly that amount.

Elden Beast drops 500,000 souls

Here is my NG+6 level 26 with +2 weapons character in Dark Souls 3

Here is me on the same character back in NG+5 with a large abundance of random amount of souls (not including the millions I can get from the consumables I had):

Pretty well known one, too, as you can find in that sub's forums. I used to troll people with super OP normally easy bosses by summoning them to OP versions of Vordt, Tree boss, etc. or most fun was a super disgustingly overpowered Mad Spirit Hodrick in the swamps when I hosted PvP. Oh, and popping a seed and watching everyone die was also hilarious. I was also known to help other people's NG+7 normal characters using this weak character for fun, and even PvPing level 120 (or above) characters using this weak character. I'm well known in both DS3 and Elden Ring community.

I do stuff like this in plenty of other games, too.

Code Vein Lv1 for entire game and all the often considered imbalanced DLC

Tales of Arise - did all the post game bosses except the super dungeon which can't be accessed early no matter what.

I probably wouldn't have screenshots like this if I were cheating:

Again, I'm well known in those communities because I already provided an immense amount of tips for PvE, PvP, builds, tactics, mechanics, etc. in them.

But yeah, I didn't cheat and having a private profile doesn't mean a person cheated. I'm pretty clearly providing in-depth tactical info in this thread about this game, too. It should be extremely obvious and, no, I'm not going to go install the game, record video by rebeating to the final boss dozens of hours later again, and then upload it to prove a nonsensical point to some random person.
label: comparative

title: Re: how do u guys beat bosses?
description:
The golden form, I just tip Light attacks, evad, get quickly 2 Focus and smash through.
Im still back on Thrusting Stance, you can start the attack from further away and quickly pull back if you miss the attack somehow.
Also its the quickest H attack imo+ for the whole animation you can stay in (relative) safe distance.
I forgot that if he traps in circle u can swing the staff to just block projectiles, I was just dodging and face tanking the dmg like silly monkey;)

Anyways its like with any fight, learn patterns and if you manage to evade bigger attacks then minor ones can be patched up anyways.
And if u face tank several major attacks no amount of debuffs help there.

I think you can also add shock/poison etc protection to the gourd/thing u drink.
GL
label: analytical
